continue_from: Repo-local validation, artifact tracking, and agent handoff scaffolding are now present on main. Continue from RR-002-T4 without inventing new UI work: the remaining honest blocker is real Windows runtime validation.
blocked_items: RR-002-T4 still requires a real Windows machine or VM to validate tray behavior, recording lifecycle, screenshot selection, and global hotkeys.
Public release publishing is still blocked until manual QA, signing/notarization inputs, and maintainer release approval are available.
next_actions: Run windows/docs/WINDOWS_VALIDATION_CHECKLIST.md on a real Windows machine or VM and update state/tasks.json, state/risks.json, and docs/roadmap/state.json with the evidence.
If a release is needed, rerun ./scripts/validate.sh, build a signed/notarized DMG, and publish only after the release gates in docs/release/release-process.md are satisfied.
For future repo work, start from ./bootstrap.sh plus the tracked state files instead of treating chat history as the handoff source.
